SnowDistribution: Firm snow - icy in places.  No visibility throughout the day above 500 metres. Winds approx 10 - 20 mph.
Comment: Even though the freezing level will be on or near the summits, the old, consolidated snowpack will remain firm and present a slip hazard for the unwary. Axe and crampons required for safe travel on the higher tops.
WeatherInfluences: Moderate South-Easterly winds. Light, intermittent showers, falling as sleety snow only on the highest tops. The freezing level will be on or just above the summits.
SnowStability: The snowpack will remain well bonded, firm and stable in all locations. Any new, moist snow deposits on the highest summits are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Moderate South-Easterly winds. Rain showers. The freezing level rose to around summit level.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. A good cover of very firm snow remains on most aspects above 700 metres with patchier cover below. A small amount of moist, fresh overnight snow observed on the higher tops, this presented no hazard. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Moist snowpack.  Cloud base was at 700 metres all day. Winds approx 20mph or less.
Comment: Some potential for falling ice from low altitude crags.
WeatherInfluences: Similar weather pattern to the previous 24 hours. A few sleety snow showers over summits with light rain at lower elevations. Freezing close to summits throughout. Light to moderate South East winds.
SnowStability: Little change. Good stability in most places. Some new moist well bonded windslab is possible on a few very steep West to North aspects above 1000m. Elsewhere the snowpack will be moist but generally well stabilised, though some localised minor wet surface snow instability may affect steeper recent lee slopes below 950m. Cornices expected to weaken. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Some overnight showers of sleety snow over summits with light rain at lower levels, then largely dry during the day. Cooler overnight then freezing near summits for a time and a little cooler later. Moderate South Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Stability is good in many places. Moist snow at all elevations. Notably well bonded snowpack at depth on most lee slopes. Some minor localised surface wet snow instability noted but restricted to a few very steep West to North aspects below 950m; the onset of cooler conditions induced some consolidation here later. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Thaw conditions at most elevations.  The summits were in the cloud most of the day.  Winds 20 -30 mph.
Comment: South-Easterly airstream continuing of the next few days.
WeatherInfluences: Some very light precipitation through the day will fall as snow above around 1000 metres. Moderate South-Easterly winds.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. New snow deposits above around 1000 metres are not expected to be significant. A gradual thaw will take place at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light rain at most elevations with wet snow above around 1150m. The freezing levels was around 1200 metres with fresh mainly Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is gradually thawing and consolidating at most elevations. Some new wet snow deposits are present above around 1150 metres.  Snowpack stability is good. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Wet snow with some lingering patches of ice. Poor visibility throughout the day above 600 metres. Winds 20-30 mph.
Comment: Continuing thaw conditions for tomorrow, looking colder from mid week.
WeatherInfluences: Fresh South-South-East winds will back South-Easterly overnight. Outbreaks of rain, falling as sleet and snow above 1000 metres. The Freezing level will be around 1000 metres.
SnowStability: Wet snow instabilities will continue to be present on steep South-West through West to North-East aspects, mainly between 600 and 1000 metres. Above this altitude fresh snow deposits are not expected to be significant. The old windslab continues to slowly consolidate but moderately bonded layers linger at depth above 1000 metres on the same aspects. Cornices will be weak and prone to collapse. Elsewhere stability will be generally good. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Showery rain persisted overnight and through the day, falling initially as sleet and then rain to the highest summits, heaviest in the morning. Strong Easterly winds backed Southerly through the day. The freezing level was above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snow pack has become wet at all levels. Stability is generally good, however surface wet snow instabilities are present above 600 metres, mainly on steep South-West through West to North-East aspects. Additionally, above 900 metres moderately bonded layers linger at depth within the old windslab. Cornices are heavy and prone to collapse. Elsewhere the snowpack continues to slowly thaw and consolidate. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack at lower elevations, becoming icy at summit level. A day of flat light and no definition - shocking visibility. Strong steady winds of around 20mph.
Comment: Slow consolidation of the snowpack is expected as the freezing level drops slightly. Some limited windslab development is anticipated as snow showers penetrate the Cairngorms from the South-East.
WeatherInfluences: Snow showers are expected above 1000 metres throughout the forecast period. Winds will generally be strong South-South-Easterlies overnight, decreasing to become fresh later. The freezing level will be around 1100 metres.
SnowStability: Fresh moderately bonded windslab will develop as the day progresses on West through North-West to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. This will be limited in extent, presenting in areas such as coire rims and gully tops. The existing snowpack will be subject to slow consolidation, but continues to present lingering weakness at depth in terrain such as coire headwalls. Lower elevations will be subject to slow thaw. Remaining cornices will be weak. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Persistent sleet and snow from around 1000 metres upwards, with rain at lower elevations. Winds were generally strong and from the South-East. The freezing level was around 1100 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack became increasingly wet as the day progressed. Saturated windslab contains lingering weaknesses at depth. This was present on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres, with areas such as gullies and coire headwalls being typical examples. Evidence of cornice debris was noted on a North-Westerly slope at 1000 metres. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Moist snowpack.  Winds approx 10 - 20 mph. Cloud base was at 700 metres for much of the day.
Comment: Precipitation amounts are not expected to be significant. Snowpack at lower levels will continue to slowly thaw and diminish.
WeatherInfluences: Very light rain is expected generally with snow on slopes mainly above 1000 metres. New snow amounts are not expected to be great.  Winds will be moderate to fresh generally South-Easterly. The freezing level will be around 1050-1100 metres through the day.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will remain soft and moist with generally good stability. At higher levels some surface new snow instabilities may exist on steep slopes, particularly gully exits and steep corrie rims. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Milder conditions have affected the area with light rain, sleet and wet snow on the highest tops. The freezing level is around 1100-1200 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is moist at all levels, stability is generally good on all but the steepest slopes where surface instabilities are possible.  The avalanche hazard is Low.
